mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, which directly compresses valuation multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in over 55 percent of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 6.5 percent signals economic contraction and reduced consumer spending, which directly lowers corporate earnings and triggers negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.5 percent for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate collapsing consumer demand, which directly reduces S&P 500 earnings expectations and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive when both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month in over 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production above 0.5 percent signals robust manufacturing output, which generates immediate positive market sentiment and short-term equity gains.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat following industrial production surges during tightening periods in more than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation outpaces the federal funds rate, real rates turn negative, eroding purchasing power and prompting central bank tightening that suppresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 200 and FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0 for three consecutive months in over 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer spending, which directly lifts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth exceeds 150 thousand monthly but 20-day forward returns are negative in over 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP signals an official recession, which triggers earnings downgrades across the S&P 500 and produces negative forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns following negative GDP growth in more than 40 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production falls while employment rises indicates labor hoarding and declining productivity, which signals economic inefficiency and triggers short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive during periods of negative industrial production combined with positive payroll growth in over 60 percent of cases.
